mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects supply-side contraction that triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of contraction periods, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative IC in post-shock regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 4.0% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation periods, or the threshold signal yields a positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, generating positive short-term equity returns as markets price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP-growth quarters, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates signals supply-driven inflation that forces monetary tightening and reduces equity valuations.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint threshold signal fails to produce a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.
